data.narrative:
Rocket Lab is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Peter Beck. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage peers: Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M). An additional 5 public tickers and 8 private peers provide sector context but are not primary inputs to the valuation.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields $6.3B, derived directly from the three-company comparable set with a range of $900M to $7.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not on file. The median valuation of $6.3B across the peer set represents the most defensible triangulation point available from the data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.4B to $7.2B, centered on $6.3B. This range reflects the spread of same-stage, same-sector comps. The primary uncertainty stems from limited methodological coverage—only one method is applicable with available data. The current market valuation of $37B+ materially exceeds this peer-derived range, a divergence attributable to factors not captured in comparable pricing (e.g., growth trajectory, contract backlog, or market sentiment premium). Revenue disclosure would enable P/S-based corroboration.
